attributes.role: witness or proof required before consecration

entities.raw_quote: ...si alicui experimento deficit documentum ante consecracionem, habeas istud preparatum…

entities.name: Vinculum spirituum

attributes.role: binding of spirits through consecration

entities.raw_quote: ...ad consecrandum vinculum spirituum et ad consecrandum omnia experimenta corrupta…

entities.name: Munich Handbook (CLM 849)

attributes.role: source manuscript for the rituals

entities.raw_quote: [No direct mention in this chunk, but context confirms CLM 849 as source]

summary: This chunk contains extensive liturgical and consecratory material from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849), focused on ritual book and experiment consecration. It features repeated invocations of divine names—many drawn from Jewish and Christian mystical traditions (e.g., Tetragrammaton, Adonay, Alpha et O)—used to empower magical operations against spirits. The texts emphasize the authority of the exorcist over demonic forces (aereas potestates, infernales principes) through sacred names and biblical allusions. Ritual steps include nine-day preparation, Confiteor recitation, aspersion with holy water, and sign of the cross. Several esoteric name-formulas appear (e.g., Stimlamathon, Otaizomos), likely reflecting transmission from Arabic or Byzantine grimoire traditions.
key_passages: Hoc factum per ix dies, erit liber consecratus, et quocienscumque aliud consecrare volueris, incipias primam oracionem legere, scilicet Deus invisibilis, cum reliquis sequentibus…
per sanctissimum nomen tuum quod scribitur 4or litteris, loth, hoth, vel hec, vel A G L A, g, e, he, van, quo audito mare regreditur, aer commutatur…
...aereas potestates et insanabilem principem per hanc oracionem consecratam, humiliter et mirabiliter constringat, vt velint vel nolint humani voluntati obediant.
per nomeni per quod Solomon astringebat dyabolos et conclusit eos, Shoeth, Hebatit, Het, Agla, Jocht, Othot, Vanecht, Nabuthi…
...exorcista voluerit in unum omnes congregare, et cum voluerit dispergat, et per sanctissimum nomen tuum… consecratur hoc experimentum
